name: Ginger Limeade
recipeIngredient: 2 cups tap water or bottled water
1 piece ginger, about the size of an index finger, cut into thick slices
1 1/4 cups fresh lime juice from ten limes (approximately 16 ounces)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, combine the sugar and 1/2 cup of water. Bring to a boil, stirring frequently over high heat.
2. Allow to boil for 1 minute to dissolve the sugar, then remove from heat and pour into a pitcher. Let cool.
3. In the same saucepan, combine the remaining 1 1/2 cups of water and ginger. Bring to a boil over high heat.
4. Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes. Remove from heat and add to the sugar syrup. Allow to cool.
5. While the ginger is simmering, juice the limes.
6. When the sugar and ginger mixture is at room temperature, add the lime juice and mix well.
7. Fill four glasses with crushed ice. Add enough of the lime juice mixture to half-fill the glasses, then fill to the top with carbonated water.
8. Serve garnished with a mint sprig if desired.
Makes 4 drinks. (Per serving: 216 calories, 0 fat, 0 cholesterol, 2 milligrams sodium.)
